configLabel: tiered — REQUESTED author+reviewers=Sonnet, architect=Opus, concurrent cap-4. ACTUAL: all agents ran Opus 4.8 (model overrides not honored by the current Workflow runtime) — this leg measures CONCURRENCY ONLY, not model tiering.

wallClockMs.source: per-agent transcript timestamps (first→last per rep); reps sequential, dispatches within a rep concurrent (cap 4)

tokens.source: sum of input+output tokens across the rep's 7 agent transcripts (cache excluded)

costUsd.source: cache-aware per-agent breakdown at Opus 4.8 $5/$25 per MTok (all agents ran Opus — see configLabel)

notes: MODEL TIERING DID NOT EXECUTE. Both opts.model='sonnet' on the workflow agent() calls and the installed agents' own 'model: sonnet' frontmatter pins (issue-author, triage-reviewer, design-reviewer) were ignored — a 3-agent probe (wf_55553c46-4c5) confirmed sonnet/haiku/default requests all ran claude-opus-4-8. Environment limitation of the 2026-07-07 session's Workflow runtime, not a repo bug.

What this leg cleanly isolates vs scenario01-baseline.json: CONCURRENCY (cap 4 vs serial) on the same model. Wall-clock 6.74 -> 4.43 min mean (-34%). Cost $3.88 -> $3.00 mean (-23%) — driven by cache warmth, not model price: concurrent dispatches land within the 5-min cache TTL, shifting ~560K tokens from 1.25x cache-writes (944K -> 381K) to 0.1x cache-reads (753K -> 1290K).
Token count ~flat (281.7K vs 296.1K mean) — consistent with RESULTS.md's finding that token count tracks fan-out volume, not config.
To re-measure the true tiering leg, re-run scenario01-tier-run.mjs with config=tiered in an environment whose subagent model resolution honors 'sonnet' (verify with a probe first).
